Requested the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly at its thirty-fourth session on the implementation of the Programme in 1978 and 1979 and, after consulting the United Nations Programme of Assistance in the Teaching, Study, Dissemination and Wider Apprehension of International Law, to make recommendations on the implementation of the Programme during the year;

10. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its thirty-fourth session the question entitled "Pro› gram of United Nations assistance in the teaching, study, dissemination and wider understanding of international law".

text_en: 32/147. Measures to prevent inter-national terrorism which endangers or annihilates an hundred human lives, or compromises Jes liber› tes fundamentals, and investigates the underlying causes of the forms of terrorism and acts of violence which originate in misere, deceptions, grievances and hope, and which may be felt by some persons to sacrifice human lives, including their own, in an attempt to bring about radical changes

text_en: Recognising the importance of inter-national cooperation for the development of measures to effectively sin these acts to occur and the importance of studying the underlying causes of these acts with a view to finding just and pacific solutions› as quickly as possible.

items_en: I. Expresses its preconceived view of the increasing number of acts of international terrorism that endanger or annihilate innocent human lives or threaten fundamental freedoms;
2. Calls upon States to continue to seek just and peaceful solutions to the problem;1

Reqffirme le  droit  inalienable a l'autodetermi› nation et a l'independance de tous les peuples soumis a des regimes coloniaux et racistes, ainsi  qu'a d'autres formes  de  domination  etrangere,  et  affirme  la  legiti› mite  de  leur lutte,  en  particulier  la  lutte  des  mouve› ments de liberation nationale, conformement aux buts et aux principes de la  Charte et aux resolutions perti› nentes  des  organes  de  !'Organisation  des  Nations Unies:

4. Condamne les  actes  de  repression  et  de  ter› rorisme  auxquels  les  regimes  coloniaux,  racistes  et etrangers continuent de se livrer en privant des peuples de  leur  droit  legitime a l'autodetermination  et a l'independance et d'autres droits de l'homme et libertes fondamentales:

5. Lance 1111  appcl aux Etats qui ne l'ont pas encore fait  pour qu'ils examinent la  possibilite de devenir par› ties aux conventions internationales existantes qui por› tent sur divers aspects du  probleme du terrorisme in› ternational:

6. Invite les  Etats a prendre  toutes  les  mesures appropriees au niveau national en vue de !'elimination rapide et definitive du  probleme, compte tenu des dis› positions du  paragraphe 3 ci-dessus:

7. Im·ite le  Comite  special  du  terrorisme interna› tional a poursuivre ses travaux conformement au man› dat a lui  confie par I' Assemblee generale, en vertu de sa  resolution  3034  (XXVII)  du  18  decembre  1972, en  etudiant  d'abord les  causes  sous-jacentes  du  ter› rorisme  international,  puis en recommandant les  me› sures pratiques propres ~1  le  combattre:

8. lm·ite les  Etats  qui  ne  l'ont pas  encore  fait a soumettre leurs observations et propositions concretes le  plus  tot  possible  au  Secretaire general  afin  de per› mettre au Comite special de mieux  s'acquitter de son mandat:
9. Prie le Secretaire  general  de  transmettre  au Comite  special  une etude analytique  sur les  observa› tions soumises par les Etats conformement au paragra› phe 8 ci-dessus:

10. Prie le Comite  special  d'examiner  Jes  ob› servations soumises par les Etats conformement au pa› ragraphe  8  ci-dessus  et  de  presenter  son  rapport a I' Assemblee generale. I ors de  sa trente-quatrieme ses› sion, en y joignant ses recommandations en vue d'une cooperation  eventuelle  pour  I' elimination  rapide  du probleme  compte  tenu  des  dispositions  du  para› graphe 3:

12. Decide d'inscrire la  question a l'ordre du jour provisoire de sa trente-quatrieme session.
items_en: 3. Reqfirms the inalienable right to self-determination› nation and the independence of all peoples subject to colonial and racist regimes, as well as other forms of alien domination, and affirms the legiti› mote of their struggle, in particular the struggle of the movements of national liberation, in accordance with the purposes and principles of the Charter and the relevant resolutions of the organs of the United Nations:

4. Condemns the acts of repression and terror that colonial, racist and foreign regimes continue to engage in by depriving peoples of their legitimate right to self-determination and independence and other fundamental human rights and freedoms:

5. Launches 1111 applica tion to States which have not yet done so to examine the possibility of becoming party to existing international conventions which address various aspects of the problem of international terrorism:
6. Invites States to take all appropriate measures at the national level with a view to the rapid and definitive elimination of the problem, taking into account the provisions of paragraph 3 above:

7. Imitates the Special Committee on International Terrorism to continue its work in accordance with the man› dat entrusted to it by the General Assembly, in accordance with its Resolution 3034 (XXVII) of 18 December 1972, by first studying the underlying causes of international terrorism and then recommending the appropriate practices to combat it:

8. the States which have not yet done so shall submit their observations and concrete proposals as soon as possible to the Secretary General in order to enable the Special Committee to better discharge its mandate:
9. Requests the Secretary-General to transmit to the Special Committee an analytical study on the observations submitted by States in accordance with paragraph 8 above:
10. Requests the Special Committee to examine the observances submitted by the States in accordance with paragraph 8 above and to submit its report to the General Assembly.
11. Requests the Secretary-General to provide the necessary means and services to the Special Committee, including the preparation of summary records of its meetings:
12. Decides to include the item in the provisional agenda of its thirty-fourth session.
